l	Z	d
d!d"
d#d$
d%d&
d'd(
d)d*
Z d+d,
Z!d-d.
j#d/k
Z$d4d0d1
Z%d2d3
Common operations on Posix pathnames.
Instead of importing this module directly, import os and refer to
this module as os.path.  The "os.path" name is an alias for this
module on Posix systems; on other systems (e.g. Windows),
os.path provides the same operations in a manner specific to that
platform, and is an alias to another module (e.g. ntpath).
Some of this can actually be useful on non-Posix systems too, e.g.
for manipulation of the pathname component of URLs.
/bin:/usr/binNz	/dev/null

path
!/usr/lib64/python3.9/posixpath.py
_get_sep)
z6Normalize case of pathname.  Has no effect under Posix
fspath)
Test whether a path is absolute)
startswith)
Join two or more pathname components, inserting '/' as needed.
    If any component is an absolute path, all previous path components
    will be discarded.  An empty last part will result in a path that
    ends with a separator.Nr
mapr9
endswith
	TypeError
AttributeError
BytesWarning
genericpath
_check_arg_types)
Split a pathname.  Returns tuple "(head, tail)" where "tail" is
    everything after the final slash.  Either part may be empty.
rfind
rstrip)
head
tailr2
	_splitext)
zJSplit a pathname into drive and path. On Posix, drive is always
    empty.Nr

z-Returns the directory component of a pathnamerD

    do nothing.
